title: Merge Sorted Logs
description:
You are given several log sources. Each source is a list of (timestamp, message) tuples sorted by timestamp in ascending order. Timestamps are strings and can be compared lexicographically (e.g., ISO 8601 strings).

Return up to n most recent log entries across all sources, sorted from newest to oldest.

Rules:
- The result must be stable: if two entries have the same timestamp, the entry from the source that appears earlier in the input list comes first; within the same source, preserve the original order.
- If n <= 0, return an empty list.
- If n is larger than the total number of entries, return all of them.
- Do not modify the input lists.
function_signature: def latest_logs(logs: list[list[tuple[str, str]]], n: int) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
